question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted network identifier that causes getBucketName() to resolve to an attacker-controlled cloud storage bucket, under the precondition that bucketName is blank and the network name is externally influenced, triggering ingestion of forged block files containing fraudulent paidStakingRewards entries, leading to theft of unpaid staking rewards from account 0.0.800?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StringUtils.isNotBlank(bucketName) fallback in getBucketName() by ensuring bucketName is whitespace-only (not blank per StringUtils semantics), causing the method to return a network-derived bucket name that resolves to an attacker-controlled bucket, under the precondition that the attacker can influence the network property, leading to ingestion of blocks with manipulated pending_reward values?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls a public cloud storage bucket with the same name as the network-derived default bucket name (returned by ImporterProperties.HederaNetwork.getBlockStreamBucketName) serve malicious block files containing inflated paidStakingRewards, under the precondition that no bucket ownership verification is performed, causing the importer to credit staking rewards that were never actually paid?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of integrity verification on the bucket name resolution in getBucketName() to perform a DNS rebinding or bucket-squatting attack, under the precondition that the network-derived bucket name is predictable and unregistered, causing the importer to download block files with forged staking reward transfers, leading to incorrect pending_reward values in entity_stake?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: BlockProperties (sourceType field)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the default sourceType = BlockSourceType.AUTO in BlockProperties to trigger automatic fallback from a healthy BLOCK_NODE source to a FILE source that reads from an attacker-influenced bucket, under the precondition that the attacker can cause the block node subscriber to fail 3 consecutive times (SourceHealth threshold), causing the importer to ingest forged block files with manipulated staking reward amounts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: BlockProperties (sourceType field)] Can an unprivileged external user craft network conditions that cause the AUTO sourceType to oscillate between BLOCK_NODE and FILE sources at staking period boundaries, under the precondition that the attacker can selectively disrupt block node connectivity, causing the importer to process duplicate or skipped staking reward transfer records, leading to double-counting or omission of pending_reward updates?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: BlockProperties (sourceType field)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BlockSourceType.AUTO fallback logic so that when blockNodeDiscoveryService.getBlockNodes() returns empty, the system silently switches to FILE source reading from getBucketName(), under the precondition that the attacker has poisoned the registered_node table or caused discovery to fail, causing staking reward blocks to be sourced from an unverified bucket?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: BlockProperties (autoDiscoveryEnabled field)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the autoDiscoveryEnabled = true default in BlockProperties to inject a malicious block node into the registered_node database table, under the precondition that the attacker can submit a NodeCreate transaction that gets processed by the importer, causing BlockNodeDiscoveryService to discover and use the attacker's node as a block source, serving blocks with fraudulent paidStakingRewards?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: BlockProperties (autoDiscoveryEnabled field)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the auto-discovery merge logic (where discovered nodes override config nodes with the same endpoint) to perform a node impersonation attack, under the precondition that the attacker registers a block node endpoint matching a legitimate config node, causing the importer to use attacker-controlled block data that omits staking reward transfers for targeted accounts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: BlockProperties (nodes field)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that nodes defaults to Collections.emptyList() and auto-discovery is enabled by default, so that when no static nodes are configured, all block nodes come from auto-discovery, under the precondition that the attacker can register a malicious block node on the network, causing all staking reward block data to flow through the attacker's node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: BlockProperties (frequency field)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 100ms default frequency in BlockProperties to cause a race condition between two concurrent block ingestion cycles at a staking period boundary, under the precondition that the attacker can time a high-volume transaction burst to straddle the UTC midnight boundary, causing stakePeriodStart to be set incorrectly and pending_reward to be calculated with the wrong epoch day?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: BlockProperties (frequency field)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the high polling frequency (100ms) of the block importer to cause repeated processing of the same block containing a NodeStakeUpdateTransaction, under the precondition that block deduplication logic has a gap, causing entity_stake.pending_reward to be accumulated multiple times for the same staking period as documented in RecalculatePendingRewardMigration?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: BlockProperties (frequency field)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fixed-delay scheduling (fixedDelayString = #{@blockProperties.getFrequency().toMillis()}) to cause a denial-of-service on staking reward processing by flooding the block node with requests, under the precondition that the attacker controls a block node that responds slowly, causing the importer to miss staking period end events and leaving pending_reward uncalculated for affected accounts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: BlockProperties (enabled field)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the enabled = false default in BlockProperties to cause a configuration where both block stream and record stream are simultaneously active during cutover, under the precondition that the attacker can trigger the cutover mechanism, causing duplicate staking reward transfer records to be inserted into staking_reward_transfer, inflating pending_reward for targeted accounts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: BlockProperties (persistBytes field)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the persistBytes = false default to prevent forensic detection of tampered block files, under the precondition that the attacker serves malicious blocks through an auto-discovered node, causing fraudulent staking reward transfers to be persisted without any raw byte evidence for auditing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: BlockProperties (writeFiles field)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the writeFiles = false default to ensure that no local copy of processed block files is retained, under the precondition that the attacker serves blocks with manipulated paidStakingRewards through a malicious block node, causing the fraudulent staking reward data to be undetectable after the fact since no file evidence is preserved?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: BlockProperties (ledger field)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the nullable LedgerProperties ledger field in BlockProperties to bypass TSS (Threshold Signature Scheme) verification of block proofs, under the precondition that ledger is not configured (null), causing the importer to accept blocks from any source without cryptographic verification of the block proof, allowing injection of blocks with fraudulent staking reward data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: BlockProperties (ledger field)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Valid annotation on the ledger field combined with a null ledger configuration to cause the block stream verifier to skip ledger ID verification, under the precondition that the deployment omits ledger configuration, causing blocks served by a malicious auto-discovered node to pass verification and have their staking reward transfers persisted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: BlockProperties (stream field)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StreamProperties.maxBlockItems = 800000 limit to craft an oversized block that causes the importer to reject a legitimate block containing a NodeStakeUpdateTransaction, under the precondition that the attacker can influence block node behavior to pad blocks with dummy items, causing the staking period end to be missed and pending_reward to remain stale for all staking accounts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: BlockProperties (stream field)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StreamProperties.maxSubscribeAttempts = 3 limit to force the importer to mark a legitimate block node as inactive after 3 induced failures, under the precondition that the attacker can cause transient network errors to the legitimate node, causing the importer to fall back to a FILE source reading from an attacker-controlled bucket containing blocks with manipulated staking reward amounts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: BlockProperties (stream field)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StreamProperties.responseTimeout = 400ms to cause legitimate block nodes to appear unresponsive by inducing network latency above 400ms, under the precondition that the attacker is a network-level adversary, causing the importer to switch to a FILE source and ingest blocks from an attacker-controlled bucket with fraudulent paidStakingRewards entries?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getBucketName() uses ImporterProperties.HederaNetwork.getBlockStreamBucketName() without any runtime integrity check, under the precondition that the attacker can perform a man-in-the-middle attack on the S3/GCS connection (e.g., via DNS spoofing), causing the importer to download block files from an attacker-controlled endpoint that serves blocks omitting staking reward transfers for victim accounts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: BlockProperties (sourceType field)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SourceHealth threshold of 3 errors in CompositeBlockSource to perform a targeted source-switching attack, under the precondition that the attacker can cause exactly 3 block node errors at the moment a NodeStakeUpdateTransaction block is being processed, causing the importer to switch to FILE source and skip the staking period end block, leaving pending_reward uncalculated?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: BlockProperties (autoDiscoveryEnabled field)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the cache invalidation in BlockNodeDiscoveryService (triggered by RegisteredNodeChangedEvent) to perform a cache poisoning attack, under the precondition that the attacker can submit rapid NodeCreate/NodeDelete transactions, causing the discovery cache to be repeatedly cleared and refilled with attacker-controlled block node endpoints that serve manipulated staking reward blocks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: BlockProperties (nodes field)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @NotNull @Valid annotation on the nodes collection to inject a BlockNodeProperties with a crafted host value that resolves to an internal network address (SSRF), under the precondition that the attacker can register a block node with an internal IP as the endpoint, causing the importer to stream blocks from an internal service that returns crafted staking reward data?
